rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY GARIBALDI VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY EMPLOYEES' ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File All Court Records Under Seal, Close All Court Hearings And To Proceed As An Anonymous Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 12, 2020, Line 1, Motion To File All Court Records Under Seal, Close All Court Hearings And To Proceed As An Anonymous Petitioner: Petitioner's motion to file all court records under seal, close all court hearings and to proceed as an anonymous petitioner is denied. While petitioner is entitled to protect the privacy of his medical records and other personal information, those interests may be protected by moving to seal appropriate portions of the administrative record and pleadings pursuant to California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551 if the showing required by those Rules is met. However, petitioner is not entitled to the vastly overbroad relief he seeks by his motion. [The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE BERG VS. C.H. REYNOLDS ELECTRIC, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Stephanie Berg'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant Ch Reynolds Electric, Inc. And Request For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2020, Line 1, Plaintiff Stephanie Berg'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant Ch Reynolds Electric, Inc. And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GHILOTTI BROS., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ghilotti Bros. Inc.'S Motion To Consolidate With Cgc-17-560034 (Stipulated Continuance)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 12, 2020, Line 6, Plaintiff Ghilotti Bros. Inc.'S Motion To Consolidate With Cgc-17-560034 Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Ghilotti Bros., Inc.'s motion to consolidate for all purposes is granted. The two cases involve the same parties, arise out of a common set of facts relating to the parties' performance under a subcontract for a construction project, and raise overlapping claims and issues. Consolidation would further efficiency, prevent duplicative litigation, and avoid inconsistent rulings. That Ghilotti Bros. has not filed its motion in the lowest numbered case makes no practical difference whatever. The parties can address the trial date in Dept. 206. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA ARMSTRONG VS. MELI BROWN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Meli Brown'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 12, 2020, Line 2, Defendant Meli Brown'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied as being moot since verified responses were served before the hearing. Sanctions denied because Defendants didn't reach out to Plaintiff's counsel before filing the motion. Defendants reserve the right to move to compel any insufficient responses via a subsequent motion. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.=(302/JPT)
